display_name: KAVACH — AEGIS v2 — xShieldAI Agent Execution Safety

product_family.role_note: Active interception before execution. DAN gate (L1–L4). HITL approval via Telegram/WhatsApp. The agent waits — the human decides.
product_family.analogy: Bridge watch officer (SOLAS) + co-pilot (ICAO): watches radar, can change course, must log every decision — not a passive recorder but an active authority

umbrella_declaration.name: Black Box for AI Agents
umbrella_declaration.pitch: Every plane has a CVR and FDR. Every ship has a VDR. There isn't one for AI agents. We are building it.

aviation_analogy.CVR: Cockpit Voice Recorder (ICAO Annex 6) — captures every word spoken on the flight deck, sealed, survives crashes
aviation_analogy.FDR: Flight Data Recorder (ICAO Annex 6) — logs every flight parameter at 25 samples/sec, tamper-sealed, mandatory since 1967

maritime_analogy.VDR: Voyage Data Recorder (SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 20) — captures bridge audio, radar image, AIS, nav state, sealed for 30 days minimum

three_layer_stack.WATCH: KavachOS (port 4855) — kernel-level seccomp-bpf filter + Falco anomaly detection. FDR equivalent — logs what the agent actually does at OS level, not what it claims.
three_layer_stack.STOP: AEGIS / KAVACH (port 4850) — DAN gate L1–L4 + HITL interception. Bridge officer equivalent — can intervene before execution, not only after.
three_layer_stack.RECORD: PRAMANA (port 4893) — SHA-256 chained receipt chain, Merkle tree (RFC 6962), Ed25519-signed STH, S3 Object Lock 7yr anchor. CVR/VDR equivalent — tamper-evident, admissible at inquiry.

umbrella_declaration.key_distinction: A black box is forensic — it answers questions after the disaster. This stack adds WATCH and STOP before the record layer. By the time anything reaches PRAMANA, KAVACH already saw it and decided. The record proves the watch was kept.
umbrella_declaration.regulatory_hook: EU AI Act Article 14 — Human Oversight. Effective 2026-08-02.
umbrella_declaration.standards: ICAO Annex 6 Part I (aviation FDR/CVR) · SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 20 (maritime VDR) · RFC 6962 (Certificate Transparency Merkle)

v1_note: v1 (@rocketlang/aegis 0.1.1) is built and running: budget governor, session tracking, dashboard. v2 adds Shield + Sandbox + DAN Gate. Built into same codebase at /root/aegis/.
can_answer: Is this agent authorised to execute this tool call?
What is the current cost and token usage for this agent?
What is the full delegation chain for this agent session?
Has this agent been quarantined and why?
What was the last known state of a force-closed agent?
Which agents are currently ZOMBIE or ORPHAN?
Is this Bash command in scope for this agent's policy?
Does this prompt contain an injection fingerprint?
What is the projected session cost at current burn rate?
Is a dangerous action pending human approval right now?
What was the outcome of the last automated restore drill?
What is the seccomp syscall profile for this trust_mask + domain combination?
Did this agent's seccomp profile drift from what was sealed at session start?
What Falco rules govern this domain's agent at runtime?
Is the receipt chain for this kernel session intact (tamper-evident)?
How many syscalls is this agent permitted to invoke?
Is service A authorized to call service B for capability X? (BMOS-Authorize)
can_do: Launch any agent under Linux seccomp-bpf kernel governance (kavachos run)
Generate deterministic seccomp profile from trust_mask + domain in < 100ms (KOS-010)
Generate domain-tuned Falco runtime monitoring rules (maritime, freight, general)
Seal every Falco violation event with PRAMANA SHA-256 receipt chain (KOS-005)
Audit session profile hash for drift — kernel enforcement evidence for EU AI Act Art. 14
Intercept every Claude Code tool call before execution (PreToolUse hook)
Enforce per-agent budget caps with cost attribution tree
Block destructive commands (DROP, TRUNCATE, rm -rf, prisma migrate reset)
Show live spend dashboard at port 4850 (v1 frontend — budget + sessions + alerts)
Detect prompt injection and sockpuppet attacks (LakshmanRekha, CA-006)
Validate HanumanG delegation chain at every Agent spawn (7 axes)
Enforce per-agent sandbox policy (tool allowlist, path scope, db scope, bash allowlist)
Quarantine rogue agents (freeze session, preserve evidence, human-in-loop release)
Detect and resolve zombie agents (dead process + RUNNING state)
Write resume manifests for force-closed agents
Intercept dangerous actions: plain-English WhatsApp/email notification before execution
Assess blast radius of dangerous actions (which services would break downstream)
Require dual-control approval for DAN-4 irreversible actions
Run automated weekly restore drills to verify backup integrity
Provide one-command panic button to freeze all running agent sessions
Display real-time traffic-light observability dashboard for non-technical operators
Authorize inter-service capability calls via BitMaskOS mask intersection in < 10ms (BMOS-Authorize)

open_source.note: AEGIS is kept as intentional product name (rocketlang/aegis, first-to-publish on npm). AegisMesh is a competitor in the same category — monitor for trademark conflict. Hedge: KAVACH is the primary external brand and travels independently of AEGIS — if AEGIS name is ever contested, KAVACH + KavachOS carry the product without rebrand. Never lead with AEGIS in external content; lead with KAVACH or KavachOS. AEGIS = the OSS engine. KAVACH = the face.
